reasoning: Bitcoin is currently trading between $100k and $115k (as of the knowledge cutoff in November 2025). Although it experienced significant volatility, flash crashes in October only drove it down to around $102k-$104k, and an early November dip brought it to just below $100k. The asset remains strong and has not approached the $90k barrier since the October 2, 2025 start date. With less than two months until the end of the year, dipping from ~$100k to $90,000 requires about a 10% drop, which is historically quite possible. However, given recent upward momentum punctuated by short-lived crashes that couldn't break $100k convincingly, a drop to $90k before Dec 31 is roughly a 20-30% chance. No evidence was found confirming a previous Binance 1m candle flash crash to $90k during the active period.
